# @handamade/tokens

OKLCH formula-based design tokens. Code is the source of truth; all artifacts generated.

## Machine-readable artifacts
- dist/resolved/{light,dark,acme,ember}.json: every token with OKLCH, hex, and derivation formula
- dist/guidance.json: variant intent, usage rules, state derivation, typography defaults, motion/layout/fonts notes, recipes
- dist/dtcg/{theme}.json: W3C DTCG format export
- dist/components/{name}.vars.css: component-token declarations (--ds-{component}-*)

## Rules for generated code
- Use semantic tokens (--ds-bg-*, --ds-fg-*, --ds-fill-*, --ds-border-*) in app CSS.
- Override --ds-{component}-* tokens for one-off component theming; never fork semantic tokens.
- Scales are pixel-true: --ds-space-8 is 8px (0.5rem). Typography: --ds-text-{size}-{lh}-{weight}.
- Themes: import base.css + {theme}.css + components.css + utilities.css; set data-ds-theme="{name}" ({light,dark,acme,ember}).

## Motion (--ds-duration-*, --ds-ease-*)
- Durations: 150/200/350/450/600ms. Easings: --ds-ease-standard, --ds-ease-in-out, --ds-ease-soft.
- Always drive transitions/animations with duration/ease tokens; never hardcode times or curves.
- Under prefers-reduced-motion, all --ds-duration-* zero out (D30) — no per-component opt-out needed.

## Layout (--ds-container-max, --ds-gutter, --ds-z-*)
- .ds-container: max-width 1312px, gutter 40px stepping to 24px under the md breakpoint.
- --ds-z-nav (100), --ds-z-overlay (1000), --ds-z-tooltip (1100).
- Breakpoints (sm 560 / md 960) are build-time constants (D31), not CSS vars: `import { breakpoints } from "@handamade/tokens/types"` for any @media-driven JS/CSS-in-JS.

## Typography: display, serif, mono
- Fluid display tier: --ds-display-{min}-{max}-{weight} (e.g. --ds-display-56-128-black), clamp-based and pixel-true at both endpoints; pair with the matching .ds-display-* utility class for tracking/uppercase.
- Serif and mono are first-class combo roles alongside sans: --ds-text-serif-{size}-{lh}-{weight} and --ds-text-mono-{size}-{lh}-{weight}, same naming shape as the sans scale.

## Scrims and borders
- --ds-scrim-soft/medium/heavy: bgPrimary-derived translucent overlays (0.25/0.35/0.82 alpha) for text-over-media legibility.
- --ds-border-faint: low-contrast hairline border (fgPrimary at 0.08 alpha), used by Card/NavBar and other bordered surfaces.

## Media tint (--ds-media-tint, .ds-media-tint)
- --ds-media-tint is a brand-defined filter chain (D35); apply the .ds-media-tint utility class to media elements to get a tinted-at-rest, true-color-on-hover/focus treatment, transitioning over --ds-duration-450 / --ds-ease-soft.

## Dark-first brands
- A brand theme can declare `base: "dark"` (see ember) so its formulas derive from the dark palette instead of light — use this for brands whose primary surface is a dark canvas rather than treating dark as an inverted afterthought.

## Brand fonts (D29)
- Font roles (sans/serif/mono/display) are brand-level. The DS ships no font files — consumers load each brand's webfonts themselves (e.g. ember: Archivo for display/sans, IBM Plex Serif/Mono).
- Brand font stacks fully apply when data-ds-theme is set on the root <html> element. In nested subtree theming, --ds-text-*/--ds-display-* combos keep the default stacks, since unregistered custom properties only substitute at :root.

## Component overrides (D34)
- A brand's theme file may declare `componentOverrides: Record<string, string>` (e.g. ember: button-font → mono, card-radius → 0, media-tint → its filter chain), emitted in the brand's own theme file (e.g. dist/ember.css) inside `@layer ds.components`. Prefer this over forking a component's CSS Module for brand-specific token values.
